title: Lichen planus: sawtooth rete ridges & colloid bodies
vignette: A 25-year-old woman comes to the office due to a 4-week history of a pruritic skin rash. The patient has no chronic medical conditions and takes no medication. Vital signs are normal. On examination, there are pink papules distributed symmetrically over the anterior surfaces of the shins and ankles. Shave biopsy reveals prominent hyperkeratosis along with a thickened granular layer. In addition, there is a bandlike infiltrate of mononuclear cells (predominantly lymphocytes) in the subjacent superficial dermis that involves the overlying epidermis. The rete ridges have a sharpened, sawtooth appearance. The basal layer of the epidermis is degenerated, with scattered colloid bodies. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

clues.note: Lichen planus is intensely itchy - one of its '5 Ps' (pruritic, purple/pink, polygonal papules and plaques). Pruritus is a feature but not specific on its own.

clues.text: pink papules distributed symmetrically over the anterior surfaces of the shins and ankles
clues.note: Symmetric papules favoring the wrists and ankles (flexural surfaces) is the classic distribution of lichen planus, and helps separate it from psoriasis (extensor knees/elbows).

clues.text: prominent hyperkeratosis along with a thickened granular layer
clues.note: Hyperkeratosis + a THICKENED granular layer (hypergranulosis) is characteristic of lichen planus. This is the opposite of psoriasis, which shows a THINNED granular layer and parakeratosis - a key discriminator.

clues.text: bandlike infiltrate of mononuclear cells (predominantly lymphocytes)
clues.note: A bandlike (lichenoid) lymphocytic infiltrate hugging the dermoepidermal junction is interface dermatitis - the histologic signature of lichen planus (CD8+ T-cell attack on basal keratinocytes).

clues.text: rete ridges have a sharpened, sawtooth appearance
clues.note: 'Sawtooth' rete ridges are pathognomonic for lichen planus. Combined with the bandlike infiltrate, this clinches the diagnosis.

clues.text: basal layer of the epidermis is degenerated, with scattered colloid bodies
clues.note: Basal layer degeneration with colloid (Civatte) bodies - apoptotic keratinocytes - is classic lichen planus and reflects the immune attack at the basal layer.

whatMatters: Pruritic violaceous/pink papules on the wrists/ankles with histology showing hyperkeratosis, HYPERgranulosis, a bandlike lymphocytic (interface) infiltrate, SAWTOOTH rete ridges, and colloid (Civatte) bodies = LICHEN PLANUS. Answer D. Psoriasis (the trap) has the opposite: thinned granular layer, parakeratosis, clubbed rete ridges, and silvery scale on extensors.
strategy: Anchor on the pathognomonic histology: sawtooth rete ridges + bandlike (lichenoid) lymphocytic infiltrate + colloid bodies + hypergranulosis = lichen planus. Then rule out psoriasis by the granular layer (thick in LP, thin in psoriasis) and rete shape (sawtooth in LP, clubbed in psoriasis). Eczematous dermatitides show spongiosis; erythema nodosum is septal panniculitis, not an epidermal rash.

eliminations.label: Atopic dermatitis - spongiosis, not lichenoid
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: a pruritic eczematous rash, usually beginning in childhood with a personal/family history of atopy (asthma, allergic rhinitis). ❌ WHY IT FAILS: biopsy shows spongiosis (epidermal edema)/acanthosis, not a bandlike lymphocytic infiltrate with sawtooth rete ridges and colloid bodies. ✅ Correct if: chronic itchy flexural eczema in an atopic patient.

eliminations.label: Contact dermatitis - localized, spongiotic
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: a pruritic eruption localized to areas of allergen/irritant exposure (e.g., nickel under a belt buckle). ❌ WHY IT FAILS: it is spongiotic and distribution follows the exposure - not symmetric papules with lichenoid interface histology. ✅ Correct if: itchy rash matching a contact exposure pattern.

eliminations.label: Erythema nodosum - panniculitis, not an epidermal rash
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: a delayed-type hypersensitivity reaction with tender inflammatory nodules on the shins. ❌ WHY IT FAILS: its pathology is SEPTAL panniculitis in the subcutaneous fat, not an epidermal/dermoepidermal interface process with sawtooth rete ridges. ✅ Correct if: tender pretibial nodules with septal panniculitis (e.g., sarcoidosis, strep, IBD).

eliminations.label: Psoriasis - thinned granular layer, clubbed rete (your pick)
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: an immune-mediated disease with cytokine-driven keratinocyte hyperproliferation: acanthosis with CLUBBED rete ridges, parakeratosis, THINNED granular layer, Munro microabscesses, and silvery scale on extensor surfaces. ❌ WHY IT FAILS: this biopsy shows a THICKENED granular layer, sawtooth rete ridges, a bandlike lymphocytic infiltrate, and colloid bodies - all lichen planus, the opposite of psoriasis. The shared Koebner phenomenon and pruritus is the trap. ✅ Correct if: well-demarcated plaques with silvery scale, parakeratosis, and clubbed rete ridges on knees/elbows.

reasoning: Pruritic pink papules symmetric on wrists/ankles in a young adult
Histology: hyperkeratosis + hypergranulosis, bandlike (interface) lymphocytic infiltrate, sawtooth rete ridges, basal degeneration with colloid bodies
This constellation is pathognomonic for lichen planus (CD8+ interface dermatitis)
mechanism: Lichen planus is an immunologically mediated, CD8+ T-cell-driven interface dermatitis directed at basal keratinocytes along the dermoepidermal junction. Clinically it produces the '5 Ps' - pruritic, purple/pink, polygonal papules and plaques - favoring flexor wrists and ankles, with possible nail, oral (Wickham striae), and genital involvement and a Koebner phenomenon. The characteristic histology includes hyperkeratosis, hypergranulosis (a thickened granular layer), a bandlike (lichenoid) lymphocytic infiltrate at the junction, sawtooth rete ridges, basal layer degeneration, and scattered colloid (Civatte) bodies representing apoptotic keratinocytes. Psoriasis is the key contrast - it shows parakeratosis, a thinned granular layer, clubbed rete ridges, and Munro microabscesses, essentially the inverse pattern.

difficulty.label: 📚 Pure knowledge gap
difficulty.message: First Aid Derm > lichen planus: 5 Ps; flexor wrists/ankles, oral Wickham striae; interface dermatitis - hyperkeratosis, hypergranulosis, bandlike lymphocytic infiltrate, sawtooth rete ridges, colloid (Civatte) bodies; vs psoriasis (parakeratosis, thinned granular layer, clubbed rete, Munro microabscesses).

difficulty.label: 🧭 Understanding the question
difficulty.message: The stem is a histology-recognition question. Pin the pathognomonic features (sawtooth rete + bandlike infiltrate + colloid bodies + hypergranulosis) to lichen planus, not psoriasis.

difficulty.label: 🔤 Vocabulary
difficulty.message: Unsure on hypergranulosis, parakeratosis, interface/lichenoid dermatitis, sawtooth rete ridges, colloid/Civatte bodies, or panniculitis? Tell me which.

difficulty.label: 🧲 Pulled by a distractor
difficulty.message: Psoriasis (your pick) shares pruritus and a Koebner phenomenon, but its histology is the OPPOSITE - thinned granular layer and clubbed rete ridges. The thickened granular layer + sawtooth rete here say lichen planus.

difficulty.label: 🖼️ Imaging / diagram
difficulty.message: The slide shows the giveaway features - sawtooth (pointed) rete ridges and a dense band of lymphocytes hugging the dermoepidermal junction with hypergranulosis above. If the photomicrograph tripped you, match those two findings to 'lichen planus' and the diagnosis follows.

difficulty.label: ✍️ Something else
difficulty.message: Bank: lichen planus = sawtooth rete + bandlike infiltrate + colloid bodies + HYPERgranulosis (5 Ps, wrists/ankles); psoriasis = clubbed rete + parakeratosis + THINNED granular layer + Munro microabscess (extensors).

keywords.front: Sawtooth rete ridges + bandlike lymphocytic infiltrate + colloid bodies
Granular layer: lichen planus vs psoriasis

keywords.back: Parakeratosis, clubbed rete ridges, Munro microabscesses, thinned granular layer

firstAid.detail: Derm > 5 Ps; interface dermatitis; sawtooth rete, hypergranulosis, colloid bodies; Wickham striae; HCV association.
firstAid.location: Derm > 5 Ps; interface dermatitis; sawtooth rete, hypergranulosis, colloid bodies; Wickham striae; HCV association.

firstAid.detail: Derm > psoriasis (parakeratosis/clubbed rete), atopic/contact dermatitis (spongiosis), erythema nodosum (septal panniculitis).
firstAid.location: Derm > psoriasis (parakeratosis/clubbed rete), atopic/contact dermatitis (spongiosis), erythema nodosum (septal panniculitis).

firstAid.detail: Derm/Path > basal layer attack -> colloid bodies; lichen planus, lupus, erythema multiforme.
firstAid.location: Derm/Path > basal layer attack -> colloid bodies; lichen planus, lupus, erythema multiforme.

memoryHook: Lichen planus = the 'saw' rash: sawtooth rete ridges with a band of lymphocytes and Civatte bodies. Psoriasis is the opposite (thin granular layer, clubbed rete).
